It makes sense that they would want to avoid the warmer waters, especially in the tropics, and head to the cooler waters. I'm also intrigued by the fact that they can be found in such a wide range of temperatures, from 7 to 16°C. It's impressive that they can adapt to such different environments.

The example of using two sets, A and B, to represent living creatures that are two-legged and can fly is also a great way to visualize the concept of intersection and union.
